prompt: A HarborOps Lodging & Supply Management System that includes end-to-end operations for a property company running multiple locations with on-site inventory and guest/host trust controls. On the Vue.js web interface, users sign in with a local username and password and land on role-based workspaces: Inventory Clerks manage receiving, issue, transfer, and stock counts across locations (for example, “Downtown Warehouse” and “Bayfront Property”), scanning barcodes and selecting lots to keep linens, amenities, and maintenance parts traceable. The UI highlights low-stock alerts when on-hand drops below a configurable safety threshold (default 10 units or 7 days of average usage, whichever is higher) and guides procurement receiving with vendor selection and a receiving record that captures unit cost in USD, pack size, and delivery date/time. Managers use a visual query center to filter inventory ledger activity by date range, location, item, vendor, and lot, customize visible columns, paginate through large datasets with responsive feedback, and export results to Excel/CSV for offline accounting. Front-desk and Host users handle post-stay reviews: guests submit a 1–5 rating across cleanliness, communication, and accuracy, add text and up to 6 images (5 MB each), optionally post a follow-up review within 7 days, and apply predefined tags; hosts can reply once within 14 days. A two-way rating and trust system also supports post-task reviews for service interactions, with clear on-screen credit impact explanations and an appeal path. For merchandising, staff configure a pricing and promotion engine with effective dates, mutual exclusions, and priority rules so checkout always shows the best offer and an itemized discount breakdown. Product search supports full-text keyword queries with category and attribute filters, sorting, suggested terms, and trending keywords visible to managers for merchandising decisions. The backend uses Express (TypeScript) to expose offline RESTful APIs consumed by the decoupled Vue.js frontend, with MySQL as the local system of record for inventory movements, lots, vendors, reviews, moderation actions, promotions, and credit histories. Inventory workflows validate that transfers cannot create negative stock, stock counts require manager approval when variance exceeds 5% or $250.00, and lot-controlled items must always record lot and expiration when applicable; every movement writes an immutable ledger entry to support traceability and aging/turnover calculations. Reviews and trust records enforce governance: sensitive-word filtering uses a locally maintained dictionary, anti-spam rate limits default to 3 reviews per user per hour, reported content is queued for Moderator triage, and appeals move through statuses with arbitration notes and final outcomes stored for audit. The promotion engine resolves conflicts deterministically by evaluating highest priority first, then choosing the maximum savings when priorities tie, while preserving a traceable “why this discount applied” history. Search and reporting performance is supported by MySQL full-text indexes and local caching with time-based invalidation (default 15 minutes) and nightly aggregation jobs for KPI dashboards (DAU, conversion, AOV, repurchase, refund rate) and review-efficiency/risk reports; scheduled report delivery is implemented as in-app downloads prepared at a chosen time (no email/SMS). Security is fully offline: passwords are hashed with per-user salts, sensitive fields like phone numbers are masked in UI and encrypted at rest, exports are watermarked with the requesting username and timestamp, and role-based access controls prevent non-admin users from viewing vendor costs, credit-rule configuration, or moderation audit trails.
